title: Stryphnodendron barbadetiman
intro: Stryphnodendron barbadetiman, also known as barbatimão, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stryphnodendron barbadetiman include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Stryphnodendron barbadetiman is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Stryphnodendron, which consists of tropical and subtropical trees native to Central and South America. The plant is often classified as a member of the legume family, Fabaceae, due to its characteristic pea-like flowers. Stryphnodendron barbadetiman is sometimes also referred to as a member of the Caesalpinioideae subfamily. The classification of the plant can vary depending on the specific taxonomic system used.

The bark of the Stryphnodendron barbadetiman has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
